description: Implement the Installation Data Model (tree/graph) and the Interactive Single-Line Diagram (SLD) Module. This is the core Phase 1 feature that transforms the app from isolated calculators into a connected installation graph. It adds: (1) a tree-based data model representing transformer -> main board -> sub-boards -> final circuits, (2) calculation propagation through the tree (cumulative Zs, voltage drop, Ik), (3) DS/HD 60364 compliance verification at every node, (4) an interactive SVG-based SLD canvas with click-only UI for adding/removing/selecting nodes, (5) integration with the existing PRODUCTS database, and (6) full trilingual support including RTL.
steps: STEP 1 - Installation Data Model: Insert a new section '// ===== INSTALLATION DATA MODEL =====' after the existing STATE section (around line 1976). Define: (a) InstallationNode class/factory with properties: id, type (transformer|main_board|sub_board|final_circuit), parentId, childIds[], name, electrical parameters (voltage, ib, in_rating, iz, ikmax, ikmin, zs, vdrop_percent, vdrop_cumulative, cable (reference to PRODUCTS.cables entry), protection (reference to MCB/MCCB/fuse), rcd, length_m, installMethod, temp, grouping, cosPhi, power_kW), compliance status object. (b) InstallationTree manager object with: nodes Map, rootId, methods for addNode, removeNode, moveNode, getPath(nodeId), getChildren(nodeId), getParent(nodeId), toJSON/fromJSON for persistence.

STEP 2 - Calculation Engine: Add propagation functions: (a) calcNodeIB(node) - compute load current from power/voltage/cosPhi. (b) calcNodeZs(nodeId) - accumulate Zs from root (transformer impedance + sum of cable impedances along path). Uses TRAFO_UK for transformer impedance, cable r/x values from PRODUCTS.cables, and the formula Zs = Ztrafo + sum(2*L*z_cable) for each segment. (c) calcNodeVdrop(nodeId) - accumulate voltage drop percentage from root. Uses sqrt(3)*IB*L*(R*cosPhi + X*sinPhi)/1000 for 3-phase, 2*IB*L*(R*cosPhi + X*sinPhi)/1000 for 1-phase. (d) calcNodeIk(nodeId) - compute Ikmax = cmax*U0/(sqrt(3)*Zs) and Ikmin = cmin*U0/(sqrt(3)*Zs*1.5) per IEC 60909 with cmax=1.05, cmin=0.95 for LV. (e) propagateAll() - recalculate all nodes from root downward.

STEP 3 - DS/HD 60364 Compliance Verification: Add verifyNode(nodeId) function checking: (a) IB <= In <= Iz (DS/HD 60364-4-43 clause 433.1), (b) Icu >= Ikmax (DS/HD 60364-4-43 clause 434.5.1), (c) Disconnection time compliance: for final circuits <=32A check Zs*Ia <= U0 where Ia = 5*In for curve B (0.4s at 230V per Table 41.1 of DS/HD 60364-4-41), for distribution circuits check 5s time (clause 411.3.2.3), (d) Cumulative voltage drop <= 4% (DS/HD 60364-5-52 clause 525), (e) k2S2 >= I2t (adiabatic, DS/HD 60364-4-43 clause 434.5.2). Return array of {rule, clause, status:'ok'|'warning'|'fail', detail}.

STEP 4 - Interactive SLD Module: Add a new module 'sld' to the navigation. Register it in: (a) T.da.modules, T.en.modules, T.fa.modules with appropriate translations ('Installationsplan'/'Installation Plan'/Persian equivalent), (b) the renderModule switch statement, (c) Create renderSLD() function that returns the full SLD module HTML. The module has: a toolbar with click-only buttons to add transformer/main board/sub-board/final circuit, an SVG canvas area, and a properties panel for the selected node.

STEP 5 - SVG SLD Canvas Rendering: Create renderSLDCanvas(tree) function that draws the installation tree as an interactive SVG single-line diagram. Use IEC 60617 inspired symbols: transformer (two overlapping circles), boards/panels (rectangle with busbar), circuit breakers (square with X), cables (lines with length annotation). Layout algorithm: vertical tree with the transformer at top, main board below, sub-boards branching left/right, final circuits at bottom. Each node is clickable (onclick selects it). Selected node gets a highlight glow. Show key parameters on each node: In, Iz, vdrop%, Ik. Color-code compliance: green border = all OK, amber = warning, red = fail.

STEP 6 - Node Properties Panel: Create renderSLDNodePanel(nodeId) function showing the selected node's details and providing click-only controls to: (a) Set node type from predefined options, (b) Select cable from PRODUCTS.cables (filtered by suitability using recommendCables), (c) Select protection device from PRODUCTS.mcbs/mccbs/fuses (filtered using recommendMCBs/recommendMCCBs/recommendFuses), (d) Set cable length via increment/decrement buttons (5m steps from 5-200m), (e) Set installation method via buttons (matching existing cableState.installMethod options: A1, A2, B1, B2, C, D1, D2, E, F, G), (f) Set temperature/grouping using existing patterns. All controls use the sel-btn click pattern (no text inputs).

STEP 7 - Add/Remove Nodes via Click: Implement: (a) sldAddNode(parentId, type) - adds a child node to the selected parent with sensible defaults (e.g., final_circuit gets 16A MCB, 2.5mm2 cable, 20m length), (b) sldRemoveNode(nodeId) - removes a node and all its children with confirmation, (c) sldSelectNode(nodeId) - sets the selected node and re-renders the panel. The toolbar buttons: '+Undertavle' (add sub-board to selected), '+Gruppe' (add final circuit to selected), 'Fjern' (remove selected). Ensure the tree always has at least a transformer + main board (cannot delete root).

STEP 8 - Default Installation Template: Create a sensible default installation on first load: 630 kVA transformer (uk=4%, Ztrafo from TRAFO_UK) -> Main board (100A MCCB, 50mm2 Cu cable, 10m) -> 3 sample final circuits (16A MCB B-curve, 2.5mm2 Cu, 25m each for lighting/sockets/cooker). Store in localStorage under 'sld_installation' key. Add sldSave()/sldLoad() for persistence.

STEP 9 - Integration with Existing Modules: Connect the SLD to existing calculator state. When a node is selected in the SLD, populate the existing module states (loadState, mcbState, cableState, vdropState) so the user can switch to those modules and see the selected node's parameters. Add a 'sync from SLD' indicator in the status bar. Also update syncAxes() to be aware of the SLD tree if it exists.

STEP 10 - RTL and Trilingual Support: Add all new UI strings to the T object for da/en/fa. Ensure the SLD canvas SVG respects dir='rtl' (flip horizontal layout for Persian). Add fa translations for: installation plan, transformer, main board, sub-board, final circuit, add/remove, compliance status messages, all DS/HD 60364 clause references.

STEP 11 - Headless Smoke Test: Create /projects/sandbox/Claude/test-sld.js that: (a) Extracts the <script> content from el-dimensionering.html, (b) Evaluates it in a minimal DOM-mocked environment (mock document, localStorage, window), (c) Tests the installation data model: create tree, add nodes, verify propagation (Zs, vdrop, Ik calculations match hand-computed values), (d) Tests compliance verification: deliberately create a non-compliant node (Iz < IB) and verify it flags the correct DS/HD 60364 clause, (e) Tests persistence (toJSON/fromJSON round-trip). Run with: node test-sld.js
acceptance_criteria: node --check on the extracted JS from el-dimensionering.html passes with no syntax errors
The headless smoke test (node test-sld.js) passes all assertions
A new 'sld' module appears in the navigation bar in all three languages
The SLD renders an interactive SVG showing transformer -> main board -> final circuits
Clicking '+Gruppe' adds a new final circuit node to the diagram
Clicking a node selects it and shows its properties panel with click-only controls
Cumulative Zs and voltage drop are computed correctly through the tree path
Non-compliant nodes (e.g. Iz < IB) are highlighted red with the specific DS/HD 60364 clause
All existing modules (load, fuse, mcb, mccb, cable, vdrop, scircuit, trafo, standards, bank) continue to work unchanged
The existing runSelfTests() still passes all tests
verification: Extract JS and run: node -e "const fs=require('fs'); const html=fs.readFileSync('el-dimensionering.html','utf8'); const m=html.match(/<script>([\s\S]*?)<\/script>/); fs.writeFileSync('/tmp/app.js',m[1]); console.log('Extracted');" && node --check /tmp/app.js
Run: node test-sld.js and confirm all tests pass

Verify the file still contains all original module render functions (renderLoad, renderFuse, renderMCB, renderMCCB, renderCable, renderVdrop, renderShortCircuit, renderTrafo, renderStandards, renderBank)
